framework.reasoning: The paper explicitly specifies PyTorch Geometric (Fey & Lenssen) for graph construction and GINConv layers, and PyTorch/Adam for optimization (Sec 4.1-4.3). Dataset is small (388 pairs, trees up to 9,500 nodes) so CUDA is beneficial but not required; the pipeline scalability test in the paper itself ran on CPU (Xeon Silver VM). Plain YAML is sufficient given the modest, flat hyperparameter surface (no nested sweep/multirun needs), so Hydra/OmegaConf would be over-engineering.

module_hierarchy.description: Parses Newick strings into directed graphs rooted at the in-degree-zero vertex, then duplicates edges to form the bidirectional PyG graph.
Extracts the 3 continuous per-node features (degree, is_leaf, root_distance) and the categorical taxonomic/species id used by the embedding layer.

public_methods.name: extract
public_methods.signature: extract(graph: networkx.DiGraph, species_id: int) -> tuple[torch.FloatTensor, torch.LongTensor]

module_hierarchy.description: PyG Dataset/DataLoader wrapper over the 388 labelled tree pairs (train/val/test split, seed 42), caching parsed PyG Data objects in memory per Sec 4.3.

public_methods.name: setup
public_methods.signature: setup(self, master_csv_path: str, seed: int = 42) -> None

module_hierarchy.description: Trainable species/taxonomic identifier embedding, vocab sized dynamically to max_id+1.

GIN encoder: N_LAYERS GINConv blocks (Linear-ReLU-Linear MLP, BatchNorm1d, ReLU), hidden_dim=128. num_gin_layers is a config flag (default 2, per SIR body-text reading) because Fig. 5 shows 3 -- kept swappable per the low-confidence (0.6) SIR flag.

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, x: torch.FloatTensor, edge_index: torch.LongTensor, batch: torch.LongTensor) -> torch.FloatTensor
public_methods.returns: [num_graphs, 128] pooled graph embedding via global add pool

module_hierarchy.description: Top-level model: shared-weight GINEncoder applied to both trees, concatenation, and MLP regression head (256->128->64->1, dropout 0.3), with output clamped at zero.

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, tree_a: torch_geometric.data.Batch, tree_b: torch_geometric.data.Batch) -> torch.FloatTensor

module_hierarchy.description: Huber loss wrapper (delta assumed 1.0 per SIR implementation_assumptions, exposed as a config value).

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, pred: torch.FloatTensor, target: torch.FloatTensor) -> torch.FloatTensor

module_hierarchy.description: Training loop: Adam(lr=1e-4, weight_decay=1e-4), ReduceLROnPlateau (patience=10), early stopping (patience=25), seed=42 splits.

public_methods.name: fit
public_methods.signature: fit(self, model, train_loader, val_loader, config) -> dict

module_hierarchy.description: MAE / RMSE / MAPE / R^2 computation plus calibration-plot and deviation-histogram data prep, matching Sec 5.3 / Fig 7-8.

public_methods.name: compute
public_methods.signature: compute(self, preds: np.ndarray, targets: np.ndarray) -> dict

module_hierarchy.description: YAML config loader/validator with dataclass-style access.

tensor_flows.name: SiameseGINRegressor.forward
steps: h_b = GINEncoder(x_b, edge_index_b, batch_b) -> [num_graphs,128]  (shared weights, same module instance as h_a)
y = Linear(256,128) -> ReLU -> Dropout(0.3) -> Linear(128,64) -> ReLU -> Dropout(0.3) -> Linear(64,1) -> [num_graphs,1]

tensor_flows.name: GINEncoder.forward
steps: for layer in range(num_gin_layers):  # default 2 (config-swappable, see risk assessment)

model.num_gin_layers: 2  # ASSUMED: SIR confidence 0.6 -- body text says 2, Fig.5 shows 3; swappable via this flag

training.adam_beta1: 0.9  # ASSUMED: not stated in paper, PyTorch default
training.adam_beta2: 0.999  # ASSUMED: not stated in paper, PyTorch default
training.max_epochs: 200  # ASSUMED: not stated in paper, bounded by early stopping
training.huber_delta: 1.0  # ASSUMED: not stated in paper, PyTorch nn.HuberLoss default

data.dataset_source: Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20476872 (Castanheira, Bugalho, Vaz -- 864 trees, 388 labelled pairs)

runtime.reason: GINConv, global_add_pool, Data/Batch objects (explicitly used by the paper)
Newick parsing (Bio.Phylo), matches paper's stated tooling
intermediate directed-graph representation before conversion to PyG edge_index
R^2, MAE/RMSE convenience metrics, stratified split utilities
calibration plot (Fig 7) and deviation histograms (Fig 8) reproduction

entrypoints.purpose: Train the Siamese GIN regressor end-to-end from a config file.

cli_args.help: Directory containing the downloaded Zenodo dataset
Where to write checkpoints and logs

entrypoints.purpose: Evaluate a trained checkpoint on in-distribution, cross-species, and size-extrapolation test regimes and reproduce Table 2 / Fig 7-8.
Predict the SPR distance for a single user-supplied pair of Newick tree files.

cli_args.help: Path to first Newick (.nwk) tree file
Path to second Newick (.nwk) tree file

risk_assessment.description: GIN layer count is ambiguous: paper text says 2 layers, Figure 5 diagrams 3. Getting this wrong changes receptive field and parameter count, directly affecting reported R^2.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose num_gin_layers as a config flag (default 2, per text). GINEncoder is written generically over a layer count so both values are one-line swappable; recommend running both and comparing against Table 2 numbers to disambiguate empirically.

risk_assessment.description: Batch size, total training epochs, and Adam beta1/beta2 are not stated anywhere in the paper.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use literature defaults (batch_size=32, max_epochs=200 bounded by early stopping patience=25, betas=(0.9,0.999)), all exposed in config.yaml with '# ASSUMED' comments so they can be tuned to match Table 2 without touching code.

risk_assessment.description: Huber loss delta (transition threshold) not given a numeric value.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default delta=1.0 (PyTorch default) exposed as config.training.huber_delta; SPR distances range from 0 to ~2000 so this may need tuning -- flagged in README.

risk_assessment.description: Full dataset (864 trees / 388 labelled pairs) is hosted externally on Zenodo (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20476872), not bundled in this repo; download requires network access and may be slow/unavailable.
risk_assessment.mitigation: data/download_dataset.py script fetches and caches the Zenodo archive; repo includes a tiny synthetic smoke-test fixture (data/toy/) so the pipeline can be sanity-checked without the full download.

risk_assessment.description: The paper does not specify GPU/CPU wall-clock training time or hardware for the GNN training itself (only for the labelling-pipeline stress test).
risk_assessment.mitigation: No mitigation needed for correctness; noted in README as a reproducibility gap outside this repo's control.

risk_assessment.description: Categorical taxonomic identifier semantics (per-species vs. per-isolate) are inferred rather than stated outright.
risk_assessment.mitigation: NodeFeatureExtractor.extract() takes an explicit species_id argument and treats it as per-species by default; documented as swappable to per-isolate indexing if the released Zenodo dataset schema clarifies otherwise.
